## Computer Science & Engineering

# About Department
﻿ Today, Computer Science and Engineering branch is at the core of innovations in artificial intelligence, robotics, data science, cybersecurity, and more. From healthcare and education to finance, agriculture, and entertainment, computer science applications are transforming every sector by enhancing productivity, automation, and decision-making. This has resulted in an increased demand for skilled and competent
Know More
- Our Vision
- Our Mission
- To be recognized as a center of excellence in the field of Computer Science and Engineering imparting holistic education catering the needs of the society.
- Impart knowledge by providing a healthy learning environment.
- Encourage the zeal to be updated in technology front and abide by ethical and moral values.
- Engage in result oriented research and innovation to cater the societal needs.

## Message from HOD

## + Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs)
The graduates of Computer Science and Engineering should have
PEO 1:
Gain the fundamental knowledge in the field of Computer Science and use this to develop effective and efficient solutions to the engineering problems.
PEO 2:
Relish learning technology in a learning friendly environment provided by the department to reach high academic accords.
PEO 3:
Be passionate towards continuous learning for being updated in current technologies.
PEO 4:
Involve in developing innovative solutions to solve various societal problems with the goal of developing a sustainable society.

## + Programme Outcomes (POs)
Graduates of the Computer Science and Engineering program are able to:
PO #
Graduate Attribute
Proposed PO Statement
PO 1
Engineering knowledge
Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems.
PO 2
Problem Analysis
Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences.
PO 3
Design/Development of Solutions
Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations.
PO 4
Conduct investigation of complex problems
Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions.
PO 5
Modern tool usage
Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations.
PO 6
The engineer and society
Demonstrate the ability of using acquired knowledge to analyze and solve societal issues.
PO 7
Environment and sustainability
Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development.
PO 8
Ethics
Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice.
PO 9
Individual and team work
Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings.
PO 10
Communication
Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as, being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions.
PO 11
Project Management and finance
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments.
PO 12
Life-long learning
Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

## + Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs)
PSO1: Be able to design, implement, test and evaluate a computer system, component or algorithm to solve a real world problem using appropriate data structures.
PSO2: Be able to successfully adapt to the changes in the field of computer science & engineering for both personal and professional growth.

## Dr. SADANANDA L
- BE in Computer Science and Engineering
- M.Tech in Network and Internet Engineering
- Ph.D in Computer Science and Engineering
- The German Patent received for “Intelligent Gloves for Pressure Measurement and Dermis Health Monitoring”  A German utility patent on 15/12/2021 for interdisciplinary invention, titled "Intelligent Gloves for Pressure Measurement and Dermis Health Monitoring" by the DeutschesPatent-und Markenamt in Germany, under Patent# 202021106449.8. The innovation relates to the development of intelligent gloves for pressure measurement and Dermis health monitoring, with the goal of providing patients with better physical treatment and a quicker recovery
- Security, Machine Learning for malware detection

## Journals
- Dr Sadananda Lingayya, et al. “ Dynamic task offloading for resource allocation and privacy-preserving framework in Kubeedge-based edge computing using machine learning .” Cluster Computing (April 2024): 1-17.
- Dr Sadananda L, Martis, J. E., Sannidhan, M. S., Pratheeksha Hegde, N (July 2024). Precision sketching with de-aging networks in forensics . Frontiers in Signal Processing , volume – 4, 1355573.
- Sadananda L, & Dr. Bolwar Aziz Musthafa, International Journal of Advanced Science and Technology (IJAST), Vol.29, No. 9s, (2020), pp. 5298-5307 entitled “ SVM based Approach for Detecting Malware in Android using Machine Learning ”
- Sadananda L, & Dr. Bolwar Aziz Musthafa, Journals of Emerging Technologies and Innovative Research (JETIR) May 2019, volume 6, Issue 5 UGC journal 2019, presented and published the paper in National Conference entitled as “Review on Analysis of Different Malware types in Android System”.
- Published paper titled “ Speech converter using Cue symbol for hearing impards” , along with my students, in International Journal of Modern Trends in Engineering and Reserch(IJMTER), ISSN(online): 2349-9745.
- Published paper titled “Gas Leakage detection and prediction using IOT” in International journal of Engineering Science and Computing (IJESC), Volume 9, Issue 4, April 2019.
- Published paper titled “Advanced labour finding web and android application” , along with my students, in International Research journal of Engineering and Technology (IRJET), Volume 6, Issue 4, April 2019.

## Conference
- Sadananda L, & Dr. Bolwar Aziz Musthafa, Journals of Emerging Technologies and Innovative Research (JETIR) May 2019, volume 6, Issue 5 UGC journal 2019, presented and published the paper in National Conference entitled as “ Review on Analysis of Different Malware types in Android System ”
- Paper presented and received certificate, entitled on “A SVM Classifier based approach for Malware Detection in Android using Machine Learning Technique” in the 2 nd International Conference on ‘Sustainable Urban Development, Resources Conservation and Food Security’ hosted from Bearys Knowledge Campus.

## Journals
- Ranjan Kumar H S , Ganesh Aithal, Surendra Shetty, “Group key Generation and Distribution using XOR Vector Space”, International Journal of Electrical Engineering and Informatics, Volume 15, Issue 3, 2023, Scopus Indexed, Q3.
- Krishna Raj Bhat, Ranjan Kumar H S Udaya Kumara Reddy, “ A novel scheme for lossless authenticated multiple secret images sharing using polynomials and extended visual cryptography” in IET Information Security, 2021 Volume 15 , issue 1 , pages 13-21, SCI and Scopus Indexed.
- Ranjan Kumar H S, Ganesh Aithal, Surendra Shetty, Multicast communication using different group key managements, International Journal of Recent Technology and Engineering , 2019, volume-8 issue 2, pages2317-2328. Scopus indexed.

- Ranjan Kumar H.S., Kini S, Akshatha L, Deeksha B.G., “Cheat immune visual cryptography for secure transmission of images “, International Journal of Engineering and Advanced Technology , 2019, volume-8 issue 5, pages 1175-1181. Scopus indexed.

- Chandrappa S, Dharmanna Lamani, Ranjan Kumar H.S . “Different Clinical Parameters to Diagnose Glaucoma Disease: A Review” in International Journal of Computer Applications (0975 – 8887), Volume 116 – No. 23, April 2015, ISBN: 973-93-80886-37-0.
- Ranjan Kumar H S, Shruthi H R, Prasanna Kumar H R (2014). “A Visual Secret Sharing Technique for Secure and Fast Transmission of Image” in International Journal of Innovative Research in Computer and Communication Engineering , ISSN (Online): 2320-9801, ISSN (Print): 3921-3927 April 2014.
- Ranjan kumar H S , Sudeepa K B, Prasanna Kumar and Ganesh Aithal (2013). “Enhanced security system using symmetric encryption and visual cryptography” in International Journal of Advances in Engineering & Technology , pp 1211-1219 ISSN: 2231-1963. July 2013.

- Kelur, S., Ranjan Kumar, H.S ., Raju, K, ” Selective Area Encryption using Machine Learning Technique”, IEEE 4 th 2019 Innovations in Power and Advanced Computing Technologies (i- PACT), Vellore Institute of Technology, 22-23 March 2019.

- Ranjan Kumar H S and Shruthi H R “Image Security Using Visual Cryptography Based on Random Grid Technique and DCT” in International Conference on Emerging Trends In Engineering (ICETE) , held at NMAM Institute of Technology, Nitte on 15’th May 2014.
- Ranjan Kumar H S, Raghunandan K R, Sudeepa K B and Ganesh Aithal.“Efficient Audio encryption algorithm for online application” in National conference on Frontiers and Advances in Information Science and Technology on 23’rd May 2013.

## Department of Business Administration (MBA)

# About Department
Welcome to the Department of Business Administration (MBA) at Shri Madhwa Vadiraja Institute of Technology and Management, Bantakal, Udupi—an institution that transcends traditional education to cultivate dynamic leaders for the ever-evolving business landscape. Nestled in a serene environment, our department is not merely an academic space; it is a vibrant community committed to nurturing holistic
Know More
- Our Vision
- Our Mission
To be a beacon of excellence in business education, transcending boundaries and nurturing future leaders who are not only adept in their respective fields but also embody a strong sense of ethical responsibility.
The mission of the Department of Business Administration is to provide a transformative learning environment that empowers students with the knowledge, skills, and values essential for success in the dynamic and competitive business world. We are committed to fostering academic excellence, holistic development, and a sense of social responsibility. Through a blend of rigorous academic curriculum, experiential learning, and industry exposure, we aim to produce graduates who are not only industry-ready but also capable of leading with integrity, embracing diversity, and contributing to sustainable business practices. Our mission is to be a centre of academic innovation, cultivating a culture of continuous learning, and preparing our students to be leaders who shape the future of business with a global perspective and a commitment to ethical leadership.

## Message from HOD

## +Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs)
- MBA is a two-year full-time programme, aimed at nurturing and training young minds with contemporary skills of management, adept in handling diverse sectors of the economy.
- The programme intends to inculcate leadership qualities in individuals to strategically position themselves in all emerging platforms of idea generation, creation of pragmatic knowledge, skills and competency development.
- The diverse course curriculum enables a high degree of academic flexibility for fostering innovation and creativity. It instils resilience and adaptability in students for facing the challenges of the contemporary business world.

## + Program Outcome (POs)
- Apply knowledge of management theories and practices to solve business problems.
- Foster analytical and critical thinking abilities for data-based decision-making.
- Ability to develop value-based leadership.
- Ability to understand, analyse and communicate global, economic, legal, and ethical aspects of business.
- Ability to lead themselves and others in the achievement of organizational goals contributing effectively to a team environment.

## Electronics & Communication Engineering

# About Department
Electronics today stands at the forefront of the rapidly expanding horizon of Science and Technology. The Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering in SMVITM was established in the year 2010, initially offering an undergraduate program with an intake of 60 students per year. The intake was increased to 120 in the academic year 2012-13. The
Know More
- Our Vision
- Our Mission
” To be recognized as a center of eminence in the field of Electronics and Communication Engineering for holistic engineering education and research on current technologies”
- Impart quality engineering education with ethics to students and transform them into leaders in technology, innovation and research.
- Provide a platform and academic atmosphere that will ensure the transfer of knowledge and skills to the students.
- Promote the overall personality development of the students through activities that have high credibility and societal impact.

## Message from HOD

## + Program Educational Objectives (PEOs)
The graduate of Electronics and Communication Engineering should be able to
PEO-1
Exhibit essential knowledge of applied sciences, mathematical modeling, logical interpretation and virtual realization to resolve real-time problems in the field of Electronics and Communication Engineering
PEO-2
Work productively as an Electronics and Communication Engineer, including supportive and leadership roles on multidisciplinary teams
PEO-3
Inculcate effective communication skills to excel in professional growth
PEO-4
Take part in lifelong learning in pace with the advancing technological society

## + Program Outcomes (POs)
Engineering Graduates will be able to:
Engineering knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems
Problem analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences
Design/development of solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations
Conduct investigations of complex problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions
Modern tool usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations
The engineer and society: Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice
Environment and sustainability: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development
Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice
Individual and team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings
PO10
Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as, being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions
PO11
Project management and finance: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments
PO12
Life-long learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change

## + Program Specific Objectives(PSOs)
PSO-1
Understand the concepts of electronics & communication engineering and its application in the fields of signal processing, control systems, embedded systems, VLSI design, networking, and communication.
PSO-2
Apply domain-specific knowledge to design, analyze, synthesize and validate real-time problems in electronics & communication engineering.

PEO 3:
Be passionate towards continuous learning for being updated in current technologies.
PEO 4:
Involve in developing innovative solutions to solve various societal problems with the goal of developing a sustainable society.

## MBA Admissions

## Master of Business Administration (MBA)

## Eligibility for MBA
Applicants are required to hold a bachelor’s degree from a recognized university, with a minimum aggregate of 50%. Final-year degree students are also eligible to apply.
The selection process for management seats is merit-based, depending upon performance in Group Discussion and Personal Interview (GDPI), as well as recognized aptitude tests such as CAT, MAT, KMAT or other national-level management entrance tests.
Government seat admissions are facilitated through the PGCET entrance examination conducted by KEA.

## Program Overview
The Master of Business Administration (MBA) at SMVITM spans two transformative years. Affiliated to Visvesvaraya Technological University (VTU), our MBA program is designed to nurture the skills required for success in today’s dynamic business landscape.

## Dual Specializations
The MBA students can choose dual specialisations in the second year, from the following:
- Marketing
- Finance
- Human Resources
- Business Analytics

## Message from HOD

## + Program Educational Objectives (PEOs)
The Mechanical Engineering graduates will be able to:
- Apply the knowledge of Science and Engineering to work in diverse fields of mechanical engineering domain.
- Design and develop mechanical engineering components and systems by the acquired technical and analytical skills.
- Function effectively in multi-disciplinary environment by developing team spirit and professional competency.
- Inculcate integrity and ethical values for social and environmental developments

## + Program Outcomes (POs)
Engineering Graduates will be able to:
Engineering knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems
Problem analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences
Design/development of solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations
Conduct investigations of complex problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions
Modern tool usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations
The engineer and society: Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice
Environment and sustainability: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development
Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice
Individual and team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings
PO10
Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as, being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions
PO11
Project management and finance: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments
PO12
Life-long learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change

## + Program Specific Outcomes(PSOs)
Engineering Graduates will be able to:
PSO1
Apply the concepts of engineering mechanics, materials science, design, manufacturing, management & CAD/CAM to develop mechanical engineering systems.
PSO2
Apply the knowledge acquired in thermal, fluid and energy systems in contributing towards sustainable societal development.

Providing Value-added, Holistic Engineering Education:
SHRI MADHWA VADIRAJA INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY & MANAGEMENT, BANTAKAL, UDUPI

## Legacy of the Sode Group of Educational Institutions
Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt, one of the eight mutts of the temple town Udupi, has its footsteps in the academic sector since the pre-independence days of India. The pontiffs of Shri Sode Mutt envisioned providing modern education to all sections of society, irrespective of caste, creed, or community, with a strong commitment towards rural and economically backward students.
In pursuit of this vision, as early as 1943, His Holiness Vishwendra Theertha Swamiji established a residential school (Sanivasa Shale) at Innanje, a small village near Shankarpura, Udupi. This pioneering initiative aimed to provide quality education to financially backward students from rural parts of the country, at a time when residential education in such remote areas was almost unheard of.
The legacy was further strengthened by His Holiness Vishwothama Theertha Swamiji, who instituted a Pre-University College and English medium schools at Innanje, thereby expanding educational opportunities at the pre-university and school levels.
His Holiness Vishwavallabha Theertha Swamiji, the present pontiff of Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt, continued the sincere efforts of his Guruji in transforming this vision into reality. In 2009, he constituted the Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt Education Trust (SSVMET) to address the growing need for affordable and quality professional education in the region.
Under the aegis of SSVMET, Shri Madhwa Vadiraja Institute of Technology and Management (SMVITM) was established in 2010 at Vishwothama Nagar, Bantakal, Udupi. Initiated with an intake of only 60 students across four engineering disciplines, SMVITM has grown into a premier institution offering undergraduate programs in five engineering branches with a total intake of 480 students - Computer Science & Engineering (CSE), Electronics & Communication Engineering (ECE), Artificial Intelligence & Data Science (AIDS), Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning (AIML), and Mechanical Engineering.
The institute also offers a postgraduate MBA program with an intake of 120 students, providing dual specializations in Business Analytics, Finance, Human Resources, and Marketing, catering to the evolving demands of industry and management education.
Upholding its commitment to inclusive education, SMVITM disburses more than ₹3 crore worth of scholarships every year to meritorious and economically backward students, ensuring equal access to quality education.
Responding to the healthcare education needs of the rural belt, the institution has recently established the Niraamaya College of Nursing and the Niraamaya College of Allied Health Sciences on the same campus, offering programs in Medical Laboratory Technology, Medical Imaging Technology, and Operation Theatre Technology.

## About SMVITM
Founded in 2010 by H. H. Shri Vishwavallabha Theertha Swamiji of Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt, Shri Madhwa Vadiraja Institute of Technology & Management (SMVITM) has quickly risen to prominence as one of the most promising engineering and management colleges in coastal Karnataka. Located in Bantakal, Udupi, SMVITM blends spiritual roots with academic excellence, offering value-based, industry-aligned technical and management education to students from all backgrounds.
Affiliated to Visvesvaraya Technological University (VTU), approved by AICTE, and recognized by the Government of Karnataka, the institute offers B.E. programs in emerging and core disciplines including Artificial Intelligence & Data Science, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning, Computer Science & Engineering, Electronics & Communication Engineering and Mechanical Engineering.
In its latest academic expansion, SMVITM established a full-fledged Department of Management Studies, in 2023, offering a 2-year MBA program under VTU, with dual specializations in Business Analytics, HR, Finance and Marketing, designed to meet modern industry and entrepreneurial demands. The program emphasizes leadership, innovation, and ethical business practices - aligned with the institute’s vision.

## What sets us apart:

# Rooted in the Rural, Ready for the World
Strong spiritual roots paired with cutting-edge innovation.

# Industry & Research Collaborations
Active industry and research collaborations with a culture of transparency, positivity, and discipline.

# Vibrant Campus Life
A green, substance-free campus with a vibrant mix of academics and extracurriculars.
At SMVITM, performance speaks louder than promotion. We focus on grooming and empowering engineers and management students who not only meet industry demands but also lead with integrity and purpose. SMVITM believes the essence of imparting great education is to create a hunger
for knowledge in the students and to make them not only to improve their intellect
in the subject domain, but also to accomplish total personality development. The
institute strives to provide students a holistic education that encourages them to
apply their minds and think ‘out of the box’ to get solutions to real timetechnological problems. As today’s industry requires well qualified and industry-
ready human resources to meet the challengers of global competitiveness, thebudding engineers are trained here to remain competitive, up-dated & at the
cutting edge always.As today, a teacher is considered not as a sage on the stage, but as a guide standing
by the side, the faculty here put all efforts to identify, bring out, nurture and
promote hidden talents of students. Our Motto is “Engineering your Career and Character with Care” :
We believe that a strong and honest character is equally important as having a
successful career. Career may change, but an unshakeable character is a necessity
for a life of contentment.
Career building of students takes place here through: Knowledge Sharing, Skill
Enhancement, Promoting Innovative mindset, Inculcating Curiosity-Creativity &
Imagination, Guiding Entrepreneurship and Rigorous Training & Mentorship
Character building is taken care through: monitoring Self-Discipline,
facilitating Leadership qualities, driving Social Responsibility & Community
Service, encouraging Folk-arts & Cultural Heritage of the region, creating
Environment Consciousness and spreading Spiritual Essence.

## Some Salient Accomplishments
- “Autonomous Institution” under VTU Belagavi, conferred by UGC, New Delhi from AY 2025–26.
- Accredited by NAAC (Second Cycle) with 3.24 CGPA and “A Grade”.
- All eligible programs re-accredited by NBA – Valid up to June 2027.
- Recognised by NIRF innovation ranking in the band of 151–300 under Innovation and Entrepreneurship.
- 5 VTU level gold medals & ranks bagged by our brilliant students.
- Madhwa Incubator for Technology, Innovation and Entrepreneurship (Mi-TiE), a unit of Sode Vadiraja
Foundation (SVF), registered as a Section 8 company under Ministry of Corporate Affairs, New Delhi,
established with clear vision to nurture start-ups and translate research and innovation into scalable
businesses.
- Ministry of MSME has recognised SMVITM as Host Institute to act as Business Incubator and has already
provided thumping grants to faculty start-ups in emerging areas.
- IIC (Institutional Innovation Council) has received 4.5-star rating and also recognized as mentor
institute with funding support by MoE.
- ‘Academic Alliance’ Partnership with prestigious TCS company – Support to FDPs, Credit/Elective
Courses, Technical Workshops, Knowledge Partner for mega technical events, Value-added Certification
Programs.
- ‘Adjunct Faculty’ from renowned MICROSOFT Company.
- Active MoUs on Academic and Research Collaboration with globally recognised MAHE Manipal and NITTE
Mangalore, Deemed to be Universities.
- Vibrant Student/Faculty Chapters of reputed, International Professional Societies like ACM
(Association for Computing Machinery), ASME (American Society of Mechanical Engineers) and IEEE
(Institute of Electrical & Electronics Engineers) facilitating international exposure and
recognition to our performing students and faculty.
- Bagged Prestigious National Level Best ISTE faculty Chapter Award as well as Best ISTE Student Chapter
awards successively since many years.
- International Recognition by reputed IEEE, USA to one of our distinguished students, for her
community service / social outreach project (IEEE STEM Champion and IEEE Communication Society
Ambassador) 2025. Also getting attractive scholarships from IEEE to selected meritorious students
every year. As a part of IEEE-EUREKA program, many performing students from IEEE Student Chapter
conduct STEM awareness sessions at various High Schools in the neighbourhood, inspiring young minds.
- State-of-the-art Student Activity Centre, Inter-disciplinary Technical Hobby Club and Centre for
Product Development (CPD) with the support from external Technology Partners are being developed to
promote creative power & innovative ideas of the students.
- Vibrant NCC Unit in the campus – One of our proud NCC student participated in Republic Day Parade
2025 at New Delhi and gave cultural performance in front of our honourable PM.
- Successfully imparted Skill Development Training to more than 150 students in 4 Job Roles (CNC
Machining Technician, Junior Software Developer, Domestic Data Entry Operator & Assistant
Surveyor) under prestigious Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana 4.0 (PMKVY) flagship scheme (2024–25).
- Our performing students are winning Individual & Overall Championships at Zonal, District,
State or even National levels in Sports, Games, Extracurricular & Cultural Competitions,
Hackathons, Ideathons, App development and Coding competitions – Won first place in PRAYAS, a
national level 5G & 6G Hackathon organised by IISc, Bangalore & Ministry of Communications,
New Delhi (2024–25).
- From Campus Concepts to Industry Impact – Backed by more than 38 active MoUs & Cutting-Edge Labs
in AI, Drones and CNC – Academic Partnership or Campus Connect programs with leading
Industries/Enterprises.
- ‘Project to Product’, ‘Research to Revenue’ initiatives – Powered by new Incubation Centre and
active Consultancy Cells – Bringing industrial work culture & connections to promote student
placements.
- Nurturing Minds, Building Personality – through NCC, NSS, IEEE, ISTE & other Student Initiatives
and Clubs – Facilitating IoT competitions, Avishkar exhibitions and Innovation Challenges.
- 15 Glorious Years of Quality – Empowering Careers with rigorous placement training in all four
years, peer-learning workshops and alumni mentorship programs.
- Curious & creative students proactively teaching young children at Atal Tinkering Labs in
other schools for which SMVITM is the mentor organization.
- 10 Student Project Ideas at the innovation/prototype level across departments presented in the
national level YUKTI Innovation Challenge 2025 organised by Ministry of Education’s Innovation Cell,
Government of India.
- 21 Inter-disciplinary Student Project Ideas submitted for AICTE Productization Fellowship (APF)
scheme 2025 supporting the vision of ATMANIRBHAR BHARAT.
- MSME sponsored Inter-disciplinary Rapid Prototyping Lab coming up in the new Incubation Centre.
- Exploring Bajaj Engineering Skills Training (BEST) scheme, a flagship CSR initiative of Bajaj Auto
Ltd., to enhance placement opportunities of meritorious students.
- KSCST / VGST sponsored Student Projects in every branch and many more.

## Roadmap Ahead
The institute fully realizes the true import of the dictum: ‘Excellence is not a destination but a journey’. Accordingly, the road map ahead for SMVITM for the next 3 to 5 years is, as follows:
- To promote creative power & innovative ideas of the students and to give them suitable infrastructure for nurturing their curiosity, imagination and hidden technical/cultural talents, developing state-of-the-art “Student Activity Centre” and “Inter-disciplinary Technical Hobby Club” is planned.
- A novel, ‘Centre for Product Development (CPD)’ with the support from external Technology Partners is envisioned to facilitate SMVITM to move towards developing simple, yet unique & innovative products using the latest technologies.
- To explore introduction of ‘Internationalization’ towards improving national/international visibility and accelerated, multi-dimensional growth.
- Planned to possess/enhance necessary infrastructure & modern lab. facilities/equipment/machinery for quality ‘Research’ works and attractive ‘Testing & Consultancy’ undertakings and also to bag external research grants/funding from private/public agencies.
- To introduce new ‘Postgraduate (MTech) programs” in emerging areas having market demand such as Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Cybersecurity, Internet of Things, Renewable Energy Systems, Climate Informatics and the like, thereby strengthening advanced academic offerings and boosting focused research activities across interdisciplinary domains.
- To establish a few “Centres of Excellence” in Emerging Technologies in collaboration with premier Industrial/R&D organizations, with a special focus on creating a dedicated Centre for mapping ancient knowledge systems with modern technological advancements, fostering interdisciplinary research, innovation, consultancy and global collaboration rooted in India's intellectual heritage.
- To formulate and execute comprehensive “Faculty and Student Welfare Policies”, including sabbaticals, mentoring programs, mental wellness support, inclusive campus infrastructure, and professional counselling services.
- To develop and execute a comprehensive strategy to significantly improve the institute’s visibility in NIRF, ARIIA, NAAC, NBA, and other such national ranking frameworks as well as in international rankings like THE or QS.
- To tap the leading companies & R&D organizations for periodical Faculty Development Training in the cutting-edge technologies, courseware redesign, and live projects on campus to bridge the workplace-campus gap – Finishing School concept to be introduced through contributing companies and distinguished alumni support.
- To focus on extended services and societal engagement through local community development projects.
- Institute-Industry Interface to be promoted through membership of recognised Industrial Associations / Professional Societies of national/international repute, regular guest lectures/workshops, visiting chairs/professorships, industry visits and industry-institute interactive meets.
- To woo the support from performing/well-settled/benevolent alumni in all developmental projects and growth plans of the institute as well as for increasing attractive merit-cum-means scholarships to the needy, deserving students.
- To enhance state-of-the-art teaching pedagogy and level of modern education – featuring AI-powered smart boards, interactive content delivery, real-time lecture capture, and personalized learning analytics to enable immersive and outcome-based education.
- To provide Professional Counselling/Training towards Competitive Exams / Higher Studies Abroad.
- To arrange Hands-on exposure workshops / Experiential learning sessions aggressively, with workshop-based courses & skill development trainings.
- To set benchmarks against reputed institutes or universities around/abroad, to draw/implement some of their best practices for enhanced visibility, sustainable market standing and higher ranking of SMVITM!

## President’s Message
Education transforms and refines the human spirit, and a refined human spirit makes that contribution to society which transforms society itself. Today’s thinking should not kill the future and hence encouragement and nurturing of innovation and inquiry should become the cornerstone of any educational endeavour.
It is our attempt through the Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt Education Trust(R) to impart professional education that bridges social gaps and empowers all segments of the society to approach the future with optimism and hope. All facilities both human and otherwise will be provided for the young minds to blossom and meet challenges with confidence. May Lord Krishna Bless You All.
Shree Shree Vishwavallabha Theertha Swamiji President

## SSVMET

## Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt Education Trust ®
Promoting Value-Based Quality Education in Rural India

## Our Genesis and Objective
Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt Education Trust is promoted by Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt, Udupi, represented by its present Head, Shree Shree Vishwa Vallabha Theertha Swamiji. The Trust was registered on 29 October, 2009 as a Charitable Trust under the Indian Trusts Act 1882. The Trust is set up with the express objective of promoting educational and research activities in the rural areas of the country. The Trust is running many educational institutions which provide value based quality education to the student community. The Trust is putting its sincere efforts to meet the objectives by establishing various education institutions.
Learn More About Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt →

## Board of Trustees
Presently the Trust is having following trustees.

## Our History and Contribution to Education

# About Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt
Udupi – a coastal city of Karnataka is well known for the temple of Lord Krishna. About 700 years ago, saint Shri Madhwacharya, (considered as third incarnation of Lord Vayu) who initiated and propagated dwaita philosophy installed an idol of Lord Krishna in Udupi. For the continuation of uninterrupted puja and other customs related to this, Shri Madhwacharya appointed eight successors. The lineage of these eight direct successors of Shri Madhwacharya is today known as “Ashta Matha” (eight mutt). One amongst these mutts is Shri Sode Vadiraja mutt. Shri Vadiraja Theertha, a saint of this mutt has contributed immensely to the propagation of Madhwa Philosophy and also for the betterment of the society irrespective of caste, creed and community. As a respect to his works, the mutt has attached his name along with it.

# A Legacy of Educational Service
Over many years, Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt was providing education in ancient Indian “gurukula” system. There were many students who have been educated in disciplines like Vedic literature, Madhwa Philosophy, Astrology etc. under the able guidance of renowned scholars in the respective areas. The mutt was providing free accommodation and free food for these students till they complete their education. Over a period of time, the pontiffs of mutt thought of having a wider vision for providing quality education not only to a specific community on Indian philosophy but also providing so called modern education for all irrespective of caste creed or community. His Holiness Vishwendra Theertha Swamiji took first step to provide quality education to the people of India by establishing a residential school at Innanje a small village near Udupi. This school was started in pre-independence days itself when it was hard for getting permission to start an educational institute by British Government. The vision of Swamiji was to provide quality education to the financially backward people of rural part of the country. This institution was named as Shri Vishnumoorthy Hayavadana Swami Higher Primary School and was established in the year 1943.
On 04 June 1943, HH Shri Vishwendra Theertha Swamiji established Shri Vishnumoorthi Hayavadana High School as a residential school with the permission from Madras Government. Out of eighteen(18) high schools of undivided Dakshina Kannada District, this was the only school which was a residential school with co-education facility. HH Swamiji started this school with the help of few devotees of Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt in about 14 acres of land in a place Innanje a small village near Udupi. Due to the popularity of this school, students from various parts of Karnataka used to come and pursue their education. The unique feature of this school is that, since inception, mid-day meals is being provided to the students at highly subsidized rates. For financially backward students even these charges were waived off.
During the tenure of His Holiness Vishwottama Theertha Swamiji, the school developed in multi-dimensions. In the year 1982 this school was upgraded to Pre University college with Commerce and Arts courses. HH Vishwottama Theertha Swamiji realized that providing quality education in English medium is the need of the hour to train students of rural parts to face the global challenges. Accordingly HH Swamiji initiated SVH English medium High school at Innanje in the year 2005. In 2006, Science course is also introduced in SVH Pre University College. Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt administration took over SVS Primary school, another Kannada medium school in Innanje in the year 2007. In the year 2011 Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt Education trust started a Higher Primary school offering classes from VI Std to VII Std.

# Establishment of SMVITM
The successor of HH Vishwendra Theertha Swamiji, His Holiness Vishwottama Theertha Swamiji of Sode Vadiraja Mutt also continued his efforts in realizing the dream his Guruji into reality. Presently the pontiff of Shri Sode Vadiraja mutt, His Holiness Viswavallabha Theertha Swamiji initiated a trust called “Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt Education Trust” in the year 2009 and today all the educational institutions managed by Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt are being administered by this trust. In the year 2010, SSVMET took up a major project of establishing an engineering college to promote the students of rural part to take up professional courses which were the need of that time. Shri Madhwa Vadiraja Institute of Technology and Management(SMVITM) , established and managed by SSVMET is functioning in Bantakal Since 2010 with the aim of providing quality education in the field of Engineering for countryside people at affordable costs and also to promote research activities of global standard. Currently more than 700 students are pursuing their engineering education in Computer Science, Electronics and Communication, Mechanical and Civil Engineering disciplines. SMVITM, though very young, in terms of infrastructure provided, it is no less than any other well established Engineering institutes of this part. The management is fully committed to provide world class quality education in engineering especially for the students of rural part and also for the financially backward students. The management is providing free education to selected meritorious and financially backward students. The management is helping the needy students by providing scholarships on its own to the maximum possible extent.

# Key Features of SSVMET Institutions
- ✓ Located in rural part of the country with the aim of reaching out to rural student community.
- ✓ State-of-the-art infrastructure for providing academically conducive environment.
- ✓ Highly qualified and committed team of faculty members.
- ✓ Spacious playgrounds for sports activities.
- ✓ Platform to extract and nurture hidden talents of the students.
- ✓ About Rs 2 Lakhs spent every year in the form of scholarships to the meritorious and needy students.
- ✓ Vehicle facility to day scholars commuting from various places.
- ✓ Proven track record in terms of students’ performance and faculty accomplishments.

# Notable Alumni
The SVH institutions have got credit of their alumni who have achieved their best in their life and few of them are globally renowned personalities today.
- Late Shri B. R. Sundaram (Indian test cricket fast bowler)
- Shri M. G. Shetty (Represented India in decathlon event in Asian Games 1970 and won silver medal)
- Dr Narayana Bhat (Scientist working for NASA , USA)
- Dr P C Shetty (Famous radiologist residing in USA)
- Late Dr B R Bhat (Prof of statistics, Karnataka University Dharawad)
- Shekhar S S Kaup (Executive Director/Walden Institute at eRevMax Technologies)
- Dr. Vedavyasa Bhat Biliyar (MD, MBBS, and BS, over 30 years of practice in Psychiatry and Child & Adolescent Psychiatry in USA)
The institutions run by “Shri Sode Vadiraja Mutt Education Trust” are well known for the value based quality education being imparted to financially backward class of rural society. These institutions are committed for the all-round development of student community and thereby contributing to the growth of the country.

## Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning

# About Department
DEPARTMENT OF ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE AND MACHINE LEARNING Welcome to the Department of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning at Shri Madhwa Vadiraja Institute of Technology and Management (SMVITM), Bantakal, Udupi. Our department is dedicated to fostering a stimulating learning environment, where students explore the frontiers of AI and machine learning, and faculty members engage in cutting-edge
Know More
- Our Vision
- Our Mission
To be recognized as a center of eminence in the field of Artificial Intelligence for holistic engineering education and research on current technologies.
- To impart quality and value based education and contribute towards the innovation of computing, expert system, Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning.
- To build a data intensive system through socio-economic aspects by promoting cross-disciplinary thinking that expands expertise in cutting edge technologies and acquire professional ethics.
- Promote the overall personality development of the students through activities that have high credibility and societal impact.

## Message from HOD

## +Program Educational Objectives (PEOs)
PEO-1
Graduates will have the ability to adapt, contribute and innovate new technologies and systems in the key domains of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning.
PEO-2
Graduate will establish himself/herself as effective professionals by solving real-world problems using investigative and analytical skills along with the knowledge acquired in the field of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
PEO-3
Graduates will have the ability to explore research areas and produce outstanding contribution in various areas of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning.
PEO-4
Graduates will be ethically and socially responsible solution providers and entrepreneurs in the field of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning.

## + Program Outcomes (POs)
PO No.
Graduate Attribute
PO Statement
Engineering Knowledge
Apply the knowledge of mathematics, Science, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems.
Problem analysis
Identify, formulate, research literature, and analyse complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences.
Design/ Development of Solutions
Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health and safety, cultural, societal and environmental considerations.
Conduct investigations of complex problems
Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions.
Modern Tool Usage
Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modelling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations.
The Engineer and Society
Apply reasoning informed by contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to professional engineering practice
Environment and Sustainability
Understand the impact of professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts and demonstrate knowledge of and need for sustainable development
Ethics
Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of engineering practice.
Individual and Team Work
Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams and in multi-disciplinary settings.
PO10
Communication
Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations and give and receive clear instructions.
PO11
Project Management and Finance
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of engineering and management principles and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments
PO12
Life-long Learning
Recognize the need for and have the preparation and ability to Engage in independent and life- long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

## + Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs)
PSO-1
Ability to apply technical and research skills to design and develop algorithms using modern software tools to provide the sustainable solutions to Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning problems related to the society and environment.
PSO-2
The capacity to build computational knowledge and project development abilities in the fields of Computer Vision, Deep Learning, Machine Learning, and Artificial Intelligence using cutting-edge tools.

## Physics

## + About Department
The Department of Physics, established in 2010, has consistently maintained an excellent academic record in both teaching and research. The department currently has four dedicated teaching faculty members and boasts state-of-the-art laboratories, including a dedicated research lab. With its well-qualified faculty, the department effectively strengthens the fundamentals of Engineering Physics for first-year students.
The department has been recognized as a research center by VTU. Currently, two faculty members hold Ph.D. degrees, and the department actively undertakes quality research projects funded by various government agencies. To date, four research scholars have successfully completed their Ph.D. degrees through the department’s research center.
The Department of Physics has successfully completed two significant research projects:
- A project valued at approximately ₹15 lakhs , funded by the Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) – Board of Research in Nuclear Sciences (BRNS), Government of India.
- Another project worth ₹4 lakhs , sanctioned by the Vision Group on Science and Technology (VGST), Government of Karnataka, under the Seed Money for Young Scientists for Research (SMYSR) scheme.

## Message from HOD

## + Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs)
The objectives of the Civil Engineering undergraduate program at SMVITM are to develop civil engineers with the following
5 years down the line after graduation, the graduates should have
- A strong foundation for becoming a technically competent human resource in the broad disciplines of Civil Engineering
- Excel in Civil Engineering profession to meet the challenges of construction industry
- Update and adopt modernization in Civil Engineering with lifelong learning
- Preserve the environment for the society by adopting sustainability in Civil engineering with professional ethics

## + Programme Outcomes (POs)
Graduates of the Civil Engineering program are able to:
PO No
Graduate Attributes
At the end of the programme the student should be able to
Engineering Knowledge
Apply knowledge of mathematics, science, and engineering to solve the Civil engineering problems
Problem Analysis
Identify the problem, analyze, formulate and apply appropriate Civil engineering techniques to obtain the solutions
Design & Development of Solutions
Design Civil Engineering structures, components or processes by considering public health and safety, societal, sustainability and environmental issues
Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems
Conduct investigations of problems, search, identify and select relevant information from the literature to provide valid conclusions.
Usage of Modern Tools
Use modern tools for solving the engineering issues associated with social and professional context
Engineer & Society
Understand the role and responsibility of a Civil Engineer and apply the knowledge for the development of society
Environment & Sustainability
Apply the concepts of Civil engineering to minimize the environmental hazards by adopting sustainability in construction activities
Ethics
Understand and apply professional ethics for the issues relevant to the Civil engineering practices.
Individual & Team Work
Work as a member of a multidisciplinary project or research teams and have an understanding of leadership in teams and organizations.
Communication
Produce engineering reports, communicate the thoughts and ideas clearly and effectively.
Project Management & Finance
Demonstrate knowledge of the civil engineering project management principles and apply them to manage construction projects
Life-long Learning
Understand the need of engaging in life-long learning to update the knowledge in Civil Engineering field

## + Program Specific Outcomes(PSOs)
PSO-1
Focus on developing and exposure to alternative/ advanced technologies
Understand, develop and implement state-of-art technologies related to civil engineering
PSO-2
Extension of knowledge and testing facilities for the society
Provide solutions to day-to-day civil engineering problems by extending the intellectual knowledge base and the testing facilities for the society

## Engineering Admission

# Admission to the First Year B.E. programs at SMVITM can be obtained in any of the following modes.

## CET (KEA) Admission
Students seeking seats through the Government quota must appear for the Common Entrance Test (CET) conducted by the Karnataka Examination Authority (KEA).
KEA Code: E206
Visit KEA Website →

## COMED-K Admission
Candidates must appear for the Entrance Test conducted by COMED-K, with seat selection based on their allotment procedures.
COMED-K Code: E123
Visit COMED-K Website →

## Management Quota
Students seeking admission through the Management Quota should directly approach the college office and are required to write an entrance test conducted by the college.

# Eligibility Criteria
Candidates should have passed II PUC / 12th Std. / Equivalent Exam with English as one of the languages and obtained a minimum of 45% of marks in aggregate in Physics and Mathematics along with Chemistry / Bio-Technology / Biology / Electronics / Computer.

# Lateral Entry (2nd Year)
Students with a three-year diploma in a respective specialization can join the second year BE course. They must apply for the “Diploma Common Entrance Test (DCET)” conducted by KEA and follow the seat allotment procedure.
Visit DCET Page on KEA Website →

## Chemistry

## + About Department
Engineering chemistry helps students to consolidate the basic principles and concepts they learnt in the PUC classes. It also equips them with a sound footing of knowledge of materials, their characteristics, analysis and manufacturing processes. The whole gamut of materials such as cement, iron ore, solar grade silicon, petroleum products, corrosion inhibitors, polymers  such as  polycarbonate, polymethyl methacrylate (plexiglass),  polyurethane,  synthetic rubbers, CNT composites etc. are currently  core engineering materials. An in-depth knowledge of their applications and importance cannot be overlooked by budding engineers. Process technology updating has turned out to be the life – line activity of a practicing engineer. Time is not far away when every chemical, worth the name, need to be manufactured by a perfectly environmental friendly green process. This challenging task demands a thorough knowledge of sophisticated catalysts, nanoparticles, bond energies and thermodynamic concepts. Air pollution in industrially and   agriculturally developed regions as also thickly populated pockets of the country is crossing all leaps and bounds. Irritant gases – free automobile exhausts has become a compelling need of the day, the alternative to which is switching on to electrical vehicles. This in turn necessitates the availability of far more efficient batteries with minimal charging time. A wise application of engineering design and   chemistry of novel materials can solve this challenge. Drinking water pollution with heavy metals and coli-form bacteria has reached alarming proportions in   water sources near the so called industrially and agriculturally developed areas and thickly populated residential areas. Very precise, sensitive and sophisticated instrumental and chemical methods of analysis are required to monitor non – permissible levels of such pollutants in water. Engineering chemistry lab experiments are specially designed to train students to do this job and suggest effective measures to provide clean potable water at a reasonable cost through reverse osmosis and electro dialysis approaches.
Department profile
The department of chemistry was established in 2010 to impart basic knowledge in engineering chemistry to all the under graduate engineering programs. A consistent and near 90% result could be kept up all these years with the help of four well qualified faculty.   Since 2014 a well-established VTU recognized research center has been functioning providing research facilities leading to a Doctoral degree in chemistry. Three research scholars have registered for the PhD programme.  Also the faculties have been very active in publishing results of their research work in peer reviewed journals. The research activities taken up by the faculty in the department include medicinal chemistry, natural products, corrosion studies, synthetic chemistry etc.

The innovation relates to the development of intelligent gloves for pressure measurement and Dermis health monitoring, with the goal of providing patients with better physical treatment and a quicker recovery
- Security, Machine Learning for malware detection

## Mrs. PREETHI M.
- B. E.
- M. Tech.

## Conference
- Published the research paper titled ‘Sentiment Classification on Women’s Safety across Indian Cities using Natural Language Processing and Machine Learning’ in the International Journal of Advanced Research in Arts, Science, Engineering & Management (IJARASEM) – 2023.
- Authored the research paper titled ‘Healthcare Connect: Appointment Manager’ , published in the International Journal of Advanced Research in Science, Communication and Technology (IJARSCT) – 2024.
- Published the research paper titled ‘Text Summarization with Heart Disease Prediction’ in the International Journal of Advanced Research in Arts, Science, Engineering & Management (IJARASEM) , Volume 10, Issue 3, May 2023.
- Published the research paper titled ‘Academic Manager with Server and Classroom Feature’ in the International Journal of Advanced Research in Science, Communication and Technology (IJARSCT) – 2024.
- Co-authored the research paper titled ‘Pillbox: A Smart Solution for Personalized Medication Scheduling and Monitoring’ published in the International Journal for Research in Applied Science & Engineering Technology (IJRASET)-2024.
- Co-authored the research paper titled “Innovative Healthcare Chatbot Using Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning” -International Journal for Research in Applied Science & Engineering Technology (IJRASET) 2024.
- Authored the research paper titled “Detection of Microcalcifications with Vessels, Using Linear Structure Analysis and Uncertainty Tracking Technique in Mammography” published in the International Journal for Research in Applied Science & Engineering Technology (IJRASET) 2024.

## FDP/Workshop Attended
- Participated in Two days Faculty Development Program on “Teaching Methodology” at MIT Kundapura during 28th & 29th January 2016.
- Attended a five-day FDP on “Real Time Software Engineering & Project Management” at NMAMIT Nitte from 18th to 22nd December 2017.
- Attended a one-day workshop on “Web Development & Applications” held at Sridevi Institute of Technology, Kenjaru on 30 th of July 2018.
- Attended a Two-day FDP on “Introduction to Python Programming” conducted by ICT academy held at NMAMIT Nitte on 26th & 27th of Dec 2018.
- 5-day online Faculty Development Program (FDP) on the theme ‘Inculcating Universal Human Values in Technical Education’, organized by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) from 17th to 21st October 2022.
- Faculty Development Program on Applied Cloud Computing for Full Stack Web Development, organized by Microsoft, SAP, and AICTE under the TechSaksham initiative, held from 6th to 10th November 2023.
- Completed the 12-module Energy Literacy Training conducted by the Energy Swaraj Foundation-03/09/2023.
- International Faculty Development Program on NLP and ChatGPT Applications, organized by SECAB Institute of Engineering & Technology in collaboration with ExcelR Edtech Pvt. Ltd., held from 14th to 19th August 2023.
- Successfully completed a one-month Faculty Development Program (FDP) on Machine Learning, organized by Skill Dzire in collaboration with AICTE – 2024.
- Successfully completed a 21-day Faculty Development Program (FDP) titled ‘Artificial Intelligence (AI) Master Class’, conducted from 24th July to 23rd August 2024, by Skill Dzire.
- Successfully completed a Faculty Development Program (FDP) on Full Stack Development using Python and Django, held at SMVITM, Bantakal from 6th May to 10th May 2024.
- Successfully completed a One-Week Faculty Development Program on “Recent Advancements in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning” held from 30th June to 4th July 2025 , organized by St. Joseph’s College of Engineering in association with STEP-NITK Surathkal and Pantech E-Learning .

## Journals
- “An Efficient Cache Management using Adaptive Buffer Mechanism in MANET”, IJCAT, ISSN: 2348 – 6090, Volume 1, Issue 4 and May 2014.

## Conference
- “Improving the Efficiency of Software Defined Network through Load Balancing Algorithms” , Proceedings of the Third International Conference on Intelligent Communication Technologies and Virtual Mobile Networks (ICICV 2021).
- “ AGRIC: A Quality Farming”, Global Transitions Proceedings (2021), International Conference on Computing System and its Applications (ICCSA-2021), doi: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.gltp.2021.08.02.
- “Fake Product Detection using Blockchain” in National Conference on Emerging Innovation in Computer Science and Technology organized by PESITM Shivamogga ISBN: 978-81-963542-0-6.

## FDP/Workshop Attended
- Ms. Rukmini Bhat B, Assistant Professor, participated in AICTE sponsored six days STTP program on “Big Image Data Processing Using Machine Learning Techniques” from 4th to 9th November 2019 organized by the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Manipal Institute of Technology, MAHE Manipal.
- Ms. Rukmini Bhat Assistant Professor, participated in a Five-day Faculty Development program-” Empowering Teaching Skills” held at SMVITM Bantakal from 27th to 31st January 2020.
- One week faculty development program on Artificial Intelligence organized by the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, AIET, in association with Computer Society of India, National Youth Council of India, and Brain O Vision Solutions India Pvt.Ltd, Hyderabad from 22-26 May 2020.
- Participated and completed successfully Online Faculty Development Programme on “Networks and Security” from 26-05-2020 to 29-05-2020 at NMAM Institute of Technology, Nitte.
- Participated in the Four-Day Online FDP on “Applied Machine Learning Using Python” from 9-6-2020 to 12-6-2020 organized by Department of ISE, NMAM Institute of Technology, Nitte.
- Participated one Week Online Faculty Development Programme on “Recent Advances and Trends in Lightweight Cryptography for IoT Security and Blockchain Technologies” from 27th-31st June 2021 at UIT-RGPV, Bhopal.
- Participated in the 5-day Online FDP on the “Natural Language Processing” organized Electronics and Communication Engineering department of SMVITM Bantakal in association with Karnataka Science and Technology Academy from 25th July to 29th July 2022.
- Participated 5-day Online FDP on the theme “Inculcating Universal Human Values in Technical Education” organized by All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) from 17th October to 21st October 2022.
- Participated and successfully completed One Week AICTE–VTU Joint Teachers Training Programme on “Introduction to Python Programming & its Applications” from 13th to 17th March 2023 Organized by Visvesvaraya Technological University, Centre for PG Studies, VIAT, Muddenahalli, Chikkaballapur.
- Participated in Five Days FDP titled “Mastering Python: Emerging Trends and Innovations” from 03 to 07, February 2025 at SMVITM Bantakal.

## Dr. RANJAN KUMAR H. S.
- B. E. in Information Science and Engineering
- M. Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering
- Ph.D (Cyber Security & cryptography)
- Best paper award in ICETE 2013
- Best paper award in ICAT 2020
- Cryptography
- PG - 4
- PhD -

## Journals
- Ranjan Kumar H S , Ganesh Aithal, Surendra Shetty, “Group key Generation and Distribution using XOR Vector Space”, International Journal of Electrical Engineering and Informatics, Volume 15, Issue 3, 2023, Scopus Indexed, Q3.
- Krishna Raj Bhat, Ranjan Kumar H S Udaya Kumara Reddy, “ A novel scheme for lossless authenticated multiple secret images sharing using polynomials and extended visual cryptography” in IET Information Security, 2021 Volume 15 , issue 1 , pages 13-21, SCI and Scopus Indexed.
- Ranjan Kumar H S, Ganesh Aithal, Surendra Shetty, Multicast communication using different group key managements, International Journal of Recent Technology and Engineering , 2019, volume-8 issue 2, pages2317-2328. Scopus indexed.
- Ranjan Kumar H S, Ganesh Aithal, Surendra Shetty,” Insights on effectiveness of secure multicast communication scheme in future wireless network”, International Journal of Advanced Trends in Computer Science and Engineering , 2019, volume-8 issue 4, pages 1159-1167. Scopus indexed.
- Ranjan Kumar H.S., Kini S, Akshatha L, Deeksha B.G., “Cheat immune visual cryptography for secure transmission of images “, International Journal of Engineering and Advanced Technology , 2019, volume-8 issue 5, pages 1175-1181. Scopus indexed.
- Ranjan Kumar H S , Fathima Safeeriya, Ganesh Aithal, Surendra Shetty Ahmed, “A survey on Keyand Keyless image encryption techniques ”, Cybernetics and Information technologies ,  2017Volume-17 issue 4, Pages 131-160 SJR=0.170,SNIP=0.425, IPP =0.289. Scopus indexed .
- Sudeepa K B, Raju K. Ranjan Kumar H S , Ganesh Aithal “New Approach for video steganography based on Randomization and parallelization” , Procedia CS, Elsevier, Volume 78, Jan 2016, pp.483-490, SJR=0.30, SNIP=0.75.
- Chandrappa S, Dharmanna Lamani, Ranjan Kumar H.S , “Segmentation of Retinal Nerve FiberLayer in Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) Images using Statistical Region Merging Technique for Glaucoma Screening” in International Journal of Computer Applications (0975 – 8887) ISBN: 0975 – 8887, pp 32-35, Volume 128 – No.10, October 2015.
- Chandrappa S, Dharmanna Lamani, Ranjan Kumar H.S . “Different Clinical Parameters to Diagnose Glaucoma Disease: A Review” in International Journal of Computer Applications (0975 – 8887), Volume 116 – No. 23, April 2015, ISBN: 973-93-80886-37-0.
- Ranjan Kumar H S, Shruthi H R, Prasanna Kumar H R (2014). “A Visual Secret Sharing Technique for Secure and Fast Transmission of Image” in International Journal of Innovative Research in Computer and Communication Engineering , ISSN (Online): 2320-9801, ISSN (Print): 3921-3927 April 2014.
- Ranjan kumar H S , Sudeepa K B, Prasanna Kumar and Ganesh Aithal (2013). “Enhanced security system using symmetric encryption and visual cryptography” in International Journal of Advances in Engineering & Technology , pp 1211-1219 ISSN: 2231-1963. July 2013.

## Conference
- Akshaya, Ranjan Kumar, H.S., Bhat, D., “A novel method to recognize object in Images using Convolution Neural Networks”, 3 rd IEEE 2019 International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Control Systems, ICCS 2019, Vaigai College of Engineering, Madhurai, 15-17 May 2019.
- Kelur, S., Ranjan Kumar, H.S ., Raju, K, ” Selective Area Encryption using Machine Learning Technique”, IEEE 4 th 2019 Innovations in Power and Advanced Computing Technologies (i- PACT), Vellore Institute of Technology, 22-23 March 2019.
- Ranjan Kumar H S , Fathimath Safeeriya, Ganesh Aithal, Surendra Shetty. “Image Encryptionbased on Pixel Transposition and Lehmer Pseudo Random Number Generation” 2 nd IEEE Sponsored International conference on recent trends in Electronics, Information & Communication Technology (RTEICT), Venkateshwara college of engineering on 19th May 2017.
- Ranjan Kumar H S and Shruthi H R “Image Security Using Visual Cryptography Based on Random Grid Technique and DCT” in International Conference on Emerging Trends In Engineering (ICETE) , held at NMAM Institute of Technology, Nitte on 15’th May 2014.
- Ranjan Kumar H S, Raghunandan K R, Sudeepa K B and Ganesh Aithal.“Efficient Audio encryption algorithm for online application” in National conference on Frontiers and Advances in Information Science and Technology on 23’rd May 2013.
- Ranjan Kumar H S, Prasanna Kumar H R, Sudeepa K B, Ganesh Aithal. “A New Visual Cryptographic Scheme Using Mixed Key Generation and Residue Number System based on Chinese Remainder Theorem” in International Conference on Emerging Trends In Engineering (ICETE), held at NMAM Institute of Technology, Nitte on 15’th May 2013.

## Dr. H. UDAYA PRASANNA
- Bachelor of Technology- Metallurgical Engineering- Mysore University
- Master of Engineering-Mechanical Engineering- Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore
- Doctor of Philosophy- Materials and Metallurgical Engineering- NITK-Suratkal-Mangalore
- Fellow of Institution of Engineers, Kolkata - M and M division
- Member of  Indian Society for Training and Development, New Delhi
- Member of Indian Society for Technical Education, New Delhi
- Fellow of Indian Institute of Production Engineers, Bangalore
- Rashtrabhasha Visharad -DBHPS, Chennai
- Music, Reading, Swimming, Yoga
Built Indentation Creep Test Machine from indigenous spare  parts at the material testing laboratory of AITM, Bhatkal for conducting creep experiments on stainless steel welds1
- PG - 1 (M.Sc.-Engineering)
- PhD - 2

## Professional Services and Contributions
Delivered lectures to college level/high school students on topics like ‘Time management’, ‘ Motivation in Life’, ‘ ‘How to stop worrying and start living’, ‘ Ways to develop skills’, ‘ Getting into entrepreneurship’ ‘ How best to prepare for examinations’, ‘ How to become employment-ready in your field’ etc.- both in English and local language, under various platforms like Institution NSS , Rotary, Lions, Yoga clubs. Arranged tutorials for the less privileged locally.

## Journals
- Udayaprasanna H., Basavaraj M.V., ‘ Effect of austempering on the dry sliding wear behaviour of parent and weld metals of AISI 9260 High Silicon Steel’, Australian Journal of Mechanical Engineering  [ review awaited]
- Udayaprasanna H., Basavaraj M.V., ‘Effect of austempering on the creep behaviour of AISI 9260 high silicon steel and its welds’, Journal of Materials Science and Engineering [review awaited]
- Udayaprasanna H., Grampurohit R.D., ‘Some aspects of mechanisms and modelling of creep behaviour of 9 Cr-1Mo steels and their welds’, Indian Journal of Research in Commerce, Management, Engineering and Applied Science-ISSN 2454-6593- Vol.2, Issue No.10.pp-19 to 21
- Udayaprasanna H., Basavaraj M.V., ‘Tribological behaviour of silicon steel based weld joints’, Indian Journal of Research in Commerce, Management, Engineering and Applied Science-ISSN 2454-6593- Vol.1, Issue No.10.pp- 63 to 68
- Udayaprasanna H., Komalapur V.et.al., ‘Pre-form optimisation of autonomous under-water vehicle propeller for minimum flash’  , Indian Journal of Research in Commerce, Management, Engineering and Applied Science-ISSN 2454-6593- Vol.1, Issue No.10.pp-15 to 18
- Udayaprasanna H., Grampurohit R.D., ‘Study of steady state creep rates of 9 Cr-1Mo steel samples at 600 C temperature and 10 kg load’, International Journal of Technological Research in Engineering , Vol.3, Issue No.9.pp-23 to 34
- Udayaprasanna H., Grampurohit R.D., ‘Microstructural investigation of aged modified 9 Cr -1 Mo steel weld joints through SEM studies’, International Journal of Research in Engineering and Technology-ISSN 2319-1163- Vol.8, Issue No.4., pp.-11 to 16
- Udayaprasanna H., Grampurohit R.D., ‘Microstructural investigation of aged modified 9 Cr -1 Mo steel weld joints and correlating it with the creep behaviour’, Indian Journal of Innovative Research in Science and Technology- Vol.2, Issue No.10.pp-17 to 23
- Udayaprasanna H., Grampurohit R.D.,’ Evaluating creep damage of aged modified 9 Cr-1 Mo steel weld joints through indentation creep test methodology’- International Journal of Emerging trends in Science and Technology-Vol.3, No. 2, pp 36-43
- Udayaprasanna H., Udupa K.R., ‘ Indentation creep studies to evaluate the mechanical properties of stainless steel welds’, Australian Journal of Mechanical Engineering- ISSN 2204-2253, Vol. 14, Issue No.1, pp. 1-5
- Udayaprasanna H., Basavaraj M. V.,’ Effect of heat treatment on wear behaviour of high silicon steels’, Elixir international Journal, ISSN 2229-712X, Vol.8, No.1 pp44-46
- Udayaprasanna H., Udupa K.R., ‘Dynamic strain ageing in AISI 316L type stainless steel as revealed by indentation creep studies’ Proceedia Engineering  ELSEVIER’-ISSN 1877-7058 , Vol.55, No.1. pp566-572
- Udayaprasanna H., Udupa K.R., ‘Comparison of creep rates of AAA 5356 alloy based aluminium silicon carbide composite and AISI 316L stainless steel’, CSIR journal of structural engineering, ISSN 0970-0137, Vol.39, no.4., pp 112-115
- Udayaprasanna H., Udupa K.R., ‘Probing the deterioration of 316L stainless steel welds due to ageing and creep by indentation creep tests’ , ELSEVIER Journal of Nuclear Engineering and Design, Vol.16, No.5, pp31-38

## Conference
- Udayaprasanna H., Basavaraj M.V., Wear behaviour of oil-cooled and sub-zero conditioned AISI 310, AISI 253 ma and AISI 410 silicon steels’ Proceedings of COMET-2015, JNNEC, Shimoga -February 2015

## FDP/Workshop Attended
- Workshop on Engineering Failure Analysis-7th to 8th August 2014- MME Department- NITK-Suratkal
- Int. conference on ‘ Creep, Fatigue and creep-fatigue interaction- series 6’ , 22nd-25th January 2012- IGCAR, Kalpakkam, T.Nadu
- International Seminar on ‘Advanced Materials and Processing- series 7’-20th to 23rd  October, BEC, Bagalkot, Karnataka
- Workshop on ‘Applications of Technologies for design in institutions and industry’, 3rd May 2010, AEC, Bhatkal, Karnataka
- National convention of ISTE, New Delhi with the theme’ Excellence in Technical Education through Innovation’ -17th to 19th December 2007 -MIT, Manipal, Karnataka
- AICTE-STTP-CEP short term course on ‘Research Methodology’-18th to 24th December 2003, IIT Madras, Chennai
- Int. conference on ‘ Creep, Fatigue and creep-fatigue interaction- series 4’ , 8th to 10th October 2003- IGCAR, Kalpakkam, T.Nadu

## Journals
- C R Ramkrishnaiah, Deepika B V, Shridhara T N , (Sept 2014), Spatial Decision support system for solid waste management in Chickmaglur City, International journal of innovative science, Volume I , Issue 7, page no 77-85
- Deepika B V, Pradeep K J, (Dec 2016) , Iron removal from drinking water using low cost adsorbents- A comparitive study, International journal of innovative research in science and Engineering and Technology, Volume 5, issue 12, 20914- 20948
- Deepika B V, Sunethra A K, Raappa, Mohammed Zahid, Sandesh Nayak, (May 2017), Assessment of water quality Index (WQI) of ground water in Udupi District, Karnataka, India, International journal of science and Engineering Development Research, Volume 2, Issue 5.
- Deepika B V, Sahithya S. Shetty, Gayathri Gopal Jathan, Sachin Ananth Naik, Tibin Baby, (May 2017), Strategies for implementing Green Technologies to SMVITM Campus, International journal of science and Engineering Development Research, Volume 2, Issue 5.
- Akshay Kotian, Pavan Kumar U, Sachin Kumar S, Srikiran N S, Deepika B V, (May 2018) Assessment of Ground Water Quality in Udupi District, Karnataka,International Journal of Scientific Development and Research , IJSDR | Volume 3, Issue 5, ISSN: 2455-2631
- Deepika B V, Ramakrishnaiah, Sadhashivaiah (may-jun 2020) Assessment of ground water quality by water quality index in Udupi district, Kanataka state, India , Test Engineering and management, ISSN: 0193-4120, vol 83, Page No. 298 – 311
